summary: Tested Mnemo memory system end-to-end. All 12 backend pytest cases pass (health, seed, CRUD, search, decay, stats, timeline, agent simulate). Frontend: Overview, Memories, Search, Timeline, Example all render correctly. The previously-broken 'Add Memory' modal works end-to-end (button labeled 'New memory' opens modal -> 'Store memory' submits -> memory persists and appears in list). Reinforce and Delete buttons functional. Live stats update in real time. Agent simulate auto-remembers triggers and returns LLM replies (verified via backend pytest).

issues: Submit button labeled 'Store memory' (not 'Add Memory') and trigger labeled 'New memory'  inconsistent with feature spec wording but functional

critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 700+ lines — consider splitting into routers (memories, search, agent, lifecycle) for maintainability
events_col grows unbounded with no TTL/index — add TTL or rolling cleanup for production
search() loads up to 2000 docs into memory and scores in Python — fine for demo, but flag for scale
Decay endpoint resets score even on hot memories that haven't decayed — the >0.001 guard mitigates this
All env variables loaded correctly; no hardcoded URLs
seed_data_creation: POST /api/demo/seed seeded 6 sample memories on first call; verified via test_seed
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests at /app/backend/tests/test_mnemo.py. Frontend Add Memory uses 'New memory' trigger and 'Store memory' submit button; placeholders are not data-testid driven — recommend adding data-testid attributes for stable automation. Live stats counter (total/hot/warm) updates correctly across pages.
